title: Numeric equality and ordering BIFs
purpose: BEAM comparison operators can be used in two contexts: as guard-test opcodes (opcodes 39-44, already implemented) and as BIF calls (when passed as function references to higher-order functions like lists:sort/2). The opcode path is complete — all six ComparisonOp variants (Lt, Ge, Eq, Ne, EqExact, NeExact) decode and dispatch correctly, backed by compare::numeric_eq() for ==/=/= and compare::cmp() for ordering. The semantics are correct: 1 == 1.0 is true (numeric coercion), 1 =:= 1.0 is false (exact), and term ordering follows the BEAM standard number < atom < reference < fun < port < pid < tuple < map < nil < list < binary. However, only four comparison BIFs are registered: <, >=, =:=, =/=. The four numeric variants ==, /=, >, =< are missing. Any code that references these operators as functions — fun erlang:'=='/2, lists:sort(fun erlang:'>'/2, L), or any Gleam code compiled to use these BIFs — fails with undef.
task: Register four missing comparison BIFs in the BIF registry: erlang:'=='/2 and erlang:'/='/2 using compare::numeric_eq(), and erlang:'>'/2 and erlang:'=<'/2 using compare::cmp(). All use Capability::Pure and follow the same pattern as the existing less_than and greater_equal BIFs.

requirements.title: Numeric equality BIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL register erlang:'=='/2 and erlang:'/='/2 as BIFs in the BIF registry with Capability::Pure. WHEN erlang:'=='/2 is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Atom::TRUE if compare::numeric_eq(left, right) is true, otherwise Atom::FALSE. WHEN erlang:'/='/2 is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Atom::TRUE if compare::numeric_eq(left, right) is false, otherwise Atom::FALSE.
acceptance: erlang:'=='/2 registered in BIF registry with Capability::Pure
Implementation: compare::numeric_eq(left, right) — returns Atom::TRUE or Atom::FALSE
erlang:'/='/2 registered in BIF registry with Capability::Pure
Implementation: !compare::numeric_eq(left, right) — returns Atom::TRUE or Atom::FALSE

requirements.title: Ordering BIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL register erlang:'>'/2 and erlang:'=<'/2 as BIFs in the BIF registry with Capability::Pure. WHEN erlang:'>'/2 is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Atom::TRUE if compare::cmp(left, right, atom_table) equals Ordering::Greater, otherwise Atom::FALSE. WHEN erlang:'=<'/2 is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Atom::TRUE if compare::cmp(left, right, atom_table) does not equal Ordering::Greater, otherwise Atom::FALSE.
acceptance: erlang:'>'/2 registered in BIF registry with Capability::Pure
Implementation: compare::cmp(left, right, atom_table) == Ordering::Greater — returns Atom::TRUE or Atom::FALSE
erlang:'=<'/2 registered in BIF registry with Capability::Pure
Implementation: compare::cmp(left, right, atom_table) != Ordering::Greater — returns Atom::TRUE or Atom::FALSE
BIF handlers need atom table access — use the same pattern as existing less_than and greater_equal BIFs

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the opcode comparison handler (already correct)
SHALL NOT modify compare::numeric_eq() or compare::cmp() (already correct)
SHALL NOT address BigInt-float numeric comparison edge cases (pre-existing limitation, separate concern)
verification: cargo test passes with all existing tests plus new comparison BIF tests
erlang:'=='/2 and erlang:'/='/2 resolve correctly via BIF registry (no undef)
erlang:'>'/2 and erlang:'=<'/2 resolve correctly via BIF registry (no undef)
Numeric coercion works correctly in BIF path (1 == 1.0 is true)
